title: Canton man charged in killing of Cambridge DPW worker, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Rayquon Brown, 29, of Canton was charged in the fatal shooting of Cambridge DPW worker Xavier Bautista on July 4.
Bautista was shot near Broadway and Norfolk Street after leaving home in his work uniform; Brown acted as a 'lookout'.
The incident has reignited debate over Cambridge's recent decision to discontinue using the ShotSpotter gun detection system.

title: Boston man dead after skydiving accident in Orange
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 28-year-old Boston man died after a parachute mishap at Jumptown Skydiving in Orange, Massachusetts on Sunday.
The accident was influenced by a gust of wind causing the man to fall about 30 feet, officials reported.
This incident marks Jumptown Skydiving's second death in a year; another fatality occurred last October due to a midair collision.

title: Lindsay Clancy back in court for final hearing before murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Lindsay Clancy's bid for testimony on postpartum mental illness was denied before her murder trial in Plymouth Superior Court.
Clancy is accused of murdering her three children, with her defense arguing postpartum psychosis and medication overuse.
Jury selection is set for July 20, with the trial expected to last four to six weeks.

title: ESPN ranks Drake Maye among NFL’s top-10 quarterbacks
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Drake Maye has been ranked No. 8 on ESPN's list of the NFL's top-10 quarterbacks.
Maye led the league in quarterback rating, yards per completion, and completion percentage last season.
Under Maye, the Patriots improved from 4-13 to a 14-3 record, reaching the Super Bowl.

title: Australian wins first-ever Saugus Wing Eating Championship
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Australian James Webb won the inaugural Saugus Wing Eating Championship by eating 7.77 pounds of wings in 10 minutes.
Webb narrowly beat 18-time Nathan's Hot Dog champion Joey Chestnut, who consumed 7.54 pounds of wings, for the title.
Massachusetts native Geoff Esper finished third at the event held at The Brook in Seabrook, New Hampshire.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Linebackers edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots' linebackers ranked third in Pro Football Focus grades under coach Mike Vrabel in 2025.
Harold Landry, Robert Spillane, and Elijah Ponder are key contributors for the Patriots' upcoming season.
Rookie Gabe Jacas remains unsigned, but is expected to be a significant addition if he joins the lineup.

title: Man dead after apparent drowning in Randolph
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A man died after an apparent drowning in Great Pond, Randolph, on Sunday afternoon, according to the Norfolk District Attorney's Office.
Randolph police and fire departments responded to a 911 call about a swimmer in distress around 4 p.m. on Pond Street.
The man was found and taken to a hospital by ambulance, where he was pronounced dead; State Police are investigating.

title: Historic Somerville Theatre and Capitol Theatre buildings hit the market
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Somerville Theatre and Capitol Theatre buildings listed for sale at $12 million each, both dating back to the 1910s.
CSB Theatres operates the theaters but doesn't own the buildings; they have a multi-year lease agreement in place.
Buildings listed as part of a portfolio including an apartment building in Somerville for $16 million by Hunneman Real Estate.

title: Matt Turner’s loan with the Revolution extended; New England has option to buy GK from Lyon
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution extended Matt Turner's loan from Olympique Lyonnais through December 31, 2026, with an option to buy.
Turner, categorized as a TAM-eligible player, is no longer a Designated Player, potentially freeing cap space for new signings.
The 32-year-old goalkeeper is credited with helping the Revolution reach fourth in the Eastern Conference in the 2026 MLS season.

title: Trooper injured in wrong-way crash on Mass. Pike in Chicopee
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Trooper, his K-9, and a driver injured in a wrong-way crash on Interstate 91 in Chicopee, Massachusetts.
Jose G. Santiago, Jr., 28, faces DUI and other charges after a Breathalyzer showed a 0.19% blood alcohol level.
Massachusetts State Police report three incidents involving strikes by wrong-way drivers since early May.
